MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN WU MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT is DENIED. No proof of service of the notice of entry of demurrer order.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN WU DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend to address in good faith substantive issues raised in the demurrer. Plaintiffs shall have 30 days (from the notice of entry of order) to file an amended complaint.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Continued To Apr-15-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Overrule Demurrer
Off Calendar Jan-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF LILIAN FENG Motion To Overrule Demurrer

is DENIED. Defendant's request to dismiss made in opposition is denied as it is not procedurally or substantively proper. The Court notes that there is no notice of entry of the demurrer order on file.

Order signed and filed this day.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Overrule Demurrer
Off Calendar Jan-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF KAREN HAO Motion To Overrule Demurrer

is DENIED. Defendant's request to dismiss made in opposition is denied as it is not procedurally or substantively proper. The Court notes that there is no notice of entry of the demurrer order on file.

Order signed and filed this day.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT SAM WU'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to address in good faith all arguments raised by the demurrer. Plaintiffs shall allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action as to Defendant Wu and address litigation privilege, if Plaintiff may plead around it in good faith. Plaintiffs shall have 30 days to file an amended complaint.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)
